title: Shell injection boundary: shlex split instead of sh -c
description:
GOOD CHOICE: /! command handler uses shlex::split() + Command::new(&argv[0]).args(&argv[1..]) instead of sh -c (event.rs 975-1000). This prevents shell metacharacters (|, ;, &&, , >) from being interpreted.

The comment explicitly marks this as a SECURITY boundary: 'SECURITY: shlex split prevents shell injection — do NOT change to sh -c'.

A contributor might suggest 'sh -c' for "full shell support", but that would enable exfiltration: /! echo hi; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa would leak the SSH key to all chat participants. The shlex boundary is intentional and load-bearing.
close_reason: Verified invariant holds; nie-5xf0 documented in DESIGN.md §16 UTF-8 cursor boundary invariant; nie-pjxx and nie-h0ci already documented in DESIGN.md
